scope: Score distribution PSI, KS, AUC, ECE across train/val/test splits; top-10 feature PSI train vs test
vintage_note: Home Credit dataset has no timestamps. Train/val/test are stratified random splits from the same static snapshot. PSI measures sampling variation, not true temporal drift.

score_distribution_psi.psi_artefact_note: IMPORTANT: train-vs-val PSI=8.10 is NOT a drift signal. The Platt calibrator was fit on val-set raw scores; it maps val/test to well-calibrated [0,1] space but does not re-calibrate train. Train raw scores are more extreme (model overfits train: AUC=0.8526) producing a ~30/18/52 GREEN/AMBER/RED band distribution vs ~90/10/0.5 on val/test. Meaningful drift metric is val-vs-test PSI=0.0002 (STABLE).

score_band_distribution_by_split.note: Train band distribution (GREEN=30.5%, RED=51.9%) is Platt artefact — calibrator maps val/test scores well but not train. Val and test band distributions are operationally relevant.

governance_observations: Train PSI is large (8.10) due to Platt calibrator artefact — calibrator fit on val only. val-vs-test PSI=0.0002 is the relevant stability metric.
AUC generalization gap: train=0.8526 → val=0.7734 → test=0.7769 (no leakage signal)
ECE is consistent across splits — calibration is not split-dependent
All top-10 feature PSIs <0.10 — no distribution shift between train and test cohorts
Score band distribution is stable: GREEN ~89-90% across all splits
Train AUC=0.8526 vs val/test ~0.775 indicates expected in-sample overfitting; regularisation (LightGBM min_child_samples, reg_lambda) limits but does not eliminate this
stress_test_note: True vintage/OOT stress test not possible without timestamps. In production deployment, monthly PSI monitoring on score distribution and top-10 features is recommended.
